suggestions: I'm grateful for the quiet peace of the morning before Fajr, when the world is still and I can gather my thoughts.
I'm thankful for that first cup of teh tarik after a long, intense morning of debugging.
I'm grateful for the relief of a clean production deployment that finally goes live without any rollbacks.
I'm thankful for the sound of rain against the window during a late-night coding session, making the room feel cozy.
I'm grateful for the rezeki of a stable job that allows me to support my parents and give back to them.
I'm thankful for that rare 'aha!' moment when a logic error that kept me up all night finally clicks into place.
I'm grateful for the weekend catch-ups with friends over a solid meal, where we can laugh about our career struggles.
I'm thankful for the patience of my mentors who helped me navigate my early days as a junior dev.
I'm grateful for the comfortable feeling of coming home to a warm, home-cooked meal after a stressful sprint.
I'm thankful for the small acts of kindness from strangers, like someone holding the lift door open when I'm carrying my laptop bag.
I'm grateful for the ability to step away from the screen and find perspective in the call to prayer echoing through the neighborhood.
I'm thankful for the weekends spent with family, where the fast-paced world of tech feels miles away.
I'm grateful for the accessibility of high-speed internet that lets me learn new frameworks and stay connected with the global dev community.
I'm thankful for the flexibility to balance my professional commitments with my spiritual and personal obligations.
I'm grateful for the feeling of accomplishment after finishing a task I once thought was way beyond my skill set.
